quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g., regions cited as '$2,176.35 on 142 units' vs '$1,414.82 on just 55 units', and the recommendation quantifies a computed '$761.53 revenue gap with North'.

specificity.why: Every recommendation names products, regions, and figures (e.g., 'Grow Gadget sales in West... close its $761.53 revenue gap with North' and 'bundling it [Sprocket] with Widget').

insight_density.why: The prose surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations, such as East 'reaching second place on only 72 units' (a per-unit outlier) and Sprocket ranking 'last in revenue despite high unit volume.'

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure—e.g., Gadget's '$2,392.00... unit price of $149.50', regional revenues with unit counts, and the recommendation's '$761.53 revenue gap with North' (2176.35−1414.82).

specificity.why: Every recommendation names products, regions, and figures—'Grow Gadget sales in West... close its $761.53 revenue gap with North' and 'bundling [Sprocket] with Widget.'

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations: East 'reaching second place on only 72 units,' Sprocket ranking 'last in revenue despite high unit volume,' and Gadget's high-price/low-volume dynamic.

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure—product revenues ($2,392.00, $2,118.94), unit prices ($149.50, $19.99, $4.75), regional revenue/units ($2,176.35 on 142 units), and the derived $761.53 North-West gap.

specificity.why: Each recommendation names concrete products and regions with figures—"Grow Gadget sales in West... could close its $761.53 revenue gap with North" and "bundling [Sprocket] with Widget".

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious observations, e.g. East "reaching second place on only 72 units" (high revenue-per-unit) and Sprocket ranking "last in revenue despite high unit volume," rather than merely restating totals.
